active_goal: Improve MemoryOS Lite v3 into a benchmark-usable Letta-style agent memory system for LongMemEval and LoCoMo, without demo-only phase completion, without hiding case-level regressions, and without enabling the v3 kernel by default.

context_bundle.summary: Phase 12 must prove the archival/RAG loop from tool write to scoped archival passage retrieval and v3 context evidence, while preserving Phase 11 LoCoMo debt visibility, v3 default, v1 fallback, and kernel opt-in.

objective: Make tool-mediated archival memory writes become source-attributed, scoped, retrievable v3 archival evidence with audited history and stale-passage protection, without treating structural archive success as benchmark promotion evidence.

allowed_now: scan current phase stale ACK/result/review artifacts
forbidden_now: writing src/tests/docs outside phase-local control artifacts

borrow_semantics_only: agent passage versus source passage invariants

red_evidence_required_before_implementat.description: After approved archive_write, a later same-session V3ContextComposer.build retrieves the written content as an archival item with source_refs, memory id metadata, and archival_eligibility.selected_passage_ids.
red_evidence_required_before_implementat.expected_current_failure: The existing test only sees the tool-result message in recent context; the written memory is not proven to become eligible archival evidence without explicit archive attachment or scope.

red_evidence_required_before_implementat.description: Update/delete on the same written archival memory updates or removes apsg_{memory_id}, so stale archival evidence cannot be selected.
Session/source/archive scoping prevents a written archival passage from leaking into unrelated sessions or unattached archives.
Only if public reports are touched, v3 no-LLM public rows expose archival eligibility/final trace without changing scoring or claiming answer-quality improvement.

lane_invocation_templates.plan_storm: codex exec --yolo -m gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" "# phase: phase-12\nRead .hermes-loop/work/phase-12/context_bundle.md before any other phase-local artifact. Then read .hermes-loop/work/phase-12/god_dispatch.json. Produce work/phase-12/brainstorm.md with 2-3 implementation options for archival/RAG memory unification, tradeoffs, chosen route, risks, and demo-only traps. Do not edit src/, tests/, docs outside work/phase-12/."
lane_invocation_templates.plan_draft: codex exec --yolo -m gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" "# phase: phase-12\nRead context_bundle.md, brainstorm.md, and god_dispatch.json. Produce work/phase-12/spec.md and work/phase-12/plan.md. Plan must be TDD-oriented RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR -> smoke -> review. Do not edit src/, tests/, docs outside work/phase-12/."
lane_invocation_templates.plan_review: codex exec --yolo -m gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" "# phase: phase-12\nRead context_bundle.md, god_dispatch.json, brainstorm.md, spec.md, and plan.md. Review against active goal, anti-demo gate, v1 fallback, v3 default, kernel opt-in, source grounding, and benchmark overfitting. Write work/phase-12/plan_review.md. If PASS, write work/phase-12/plan_final.md."
lane_invocation_templates.execute: codex exec --yolo -m gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="medium" "# phase: phase-12\nRead context_bundle.md and plan_final.md before any code changes. Execute with TDD: add RED tests first, verify RED, implement minimal GREEN, refactor, run focused tests and required smoke. Write work/phase-12/result.md and execute_review.md."
lane_invocation_templates.review: codex exec --yolo -m gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="high" "# phase: phase-12\nRead context_bundle.md, god_dispatch.json, plan_final.md, result.md, execute_review.md, and git diff. Review read-only. Write work/phase-12/reviews/codex-review-phase-12.md and work/phase-12/review_verdict.json."

focused_verification_commands: uv run pytest tests/test_agent_kernel.py tests/test_archival_store.py tests/test_memory_lifecycle.py tests/test_context_composer.py -q
structural_smoke_commands: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark locomo --data-path benchmarks/locomo/locomo10.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 5 --no-llm-answer --no-llm-judge

milestone_eval_policy.longmemeval_30: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark longmemeval --data-path benchmarks/longmemeval/longmemeval.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 30
milestone_eval_policy.locomo_30: MEMORYOS_MEMORY_ARCH=v3 uv run memoryos eval public --benchmark locomo --data-path benchmarks/locomo/locomo10.json --baseline memoryos_lite --limit 30
milestone_eval_policy.required_only_if: Phase 12 changes default v3 public benchmark context behavior; otherwise case_level_eval.limit may be 0 with a written not_applicable rationale.

usable_ack_checklist: Every Phase 12 artifact has phase binding.
Plan, execute, and review artifacts cite work/phase-12/context_bundle.md.
At least one RED test failed before production code changed.
archive_write or equivalent archival write is connected to ArchivalMemory, bridged ArchivalPassage, scoped retrieval eligibility, v3 archival context item, and source refs.
Update/delete history prevents stale bridged passages from being selected.
No sourceless archival mutation is accepted.
Remaining Phase 11 LoCoMo debt is listed and not hidden by structural archival success.
MEMORYOS_AGENT_KERNEL=v1 remains opt-in and is not enabled by default.
If public benchmark context is touched, LoCoMo and LongMemEval evidence are reported separately with pass-to-fail lists.
review_focus: Does the implementation exercise the real tool/store/passage/retrieval/v3 context path?
Did tests fail before archival behavior changed?
Does the fix use generic scope/provenance semantics rather than case ids or expected answers?
Can written archival memory leak into unrelated sessions or unattached archives?
Do update/delete operations prevent stale archival evidence from being selected?
Are source refs preserved into ContextLayerItem, final_context_trace, and legacy retrieved_evidence?
Did any change alter v1 fallback, v3 default, or kernel default-off behavior?
Did the phase avoid claiming benchmark improvement from structural no-LLM evidence?
